summary: Dependency-confusion beacon to webhook.site on import
details: cryptoco-auth is an empty-shell npm package whose only behavior is an unconditional HTTPS POST to https://webhook.site/28782833-1a36-4e62-b757-772e8cbd77a1 at require/import time (index.js line 3). The package ships no README, no description, no author, and no advertised functionality — only a 7-line beacon. The name resembles legitimate authentication packages, matching the dependency-confusion / namespace-squat reconnaissance pattern: any installer that pulls this package leaks their IP and request metadata to an attacker-controlled public request-bin, confirming successful execution in the target environment and identifying organizations vulnerable to a follow-on namespace-confusion attack against an internal package of similar name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
